portfolio: Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sport and the Arts (note: source metadata incorrectly labelled this as 'Services Australia / Finance')
executive_summary: The supplied document is the Airservices Australia Corporate Plan 2025-26, not Services Australia (the metadata is wrong). Airservices is a corporate Commonwealth entity and Australia's air traffic management and aviation rescue fire fighting (ARFF) provider, operating with own-source 'airways' revenue under ACCC price oversight. The dominant theme this year is financial recovery: it is running an EBITDA loss in FY2024-25 (-$49.3m), heavily geared (87%), and has committed to the Minister to return to profitability and pay a dividend by FY2027-28, underpinned by a new long-term ACCC pricing agreement and an independent capital structure review. Simultaneously it is delivering large, multi-year transformation programs — OneSKY/CMATS, Western Sydney International, ARFF NexGen, the Enterprise Network Modernisation Program and digital aerodrome services — while uplifting culture (engagement 59%), workforce pipelines (ATC and firefighter trainees), cyber resilience and community-noise engagement. This combination of acute financial pressure plus a heavy capital/transformation portfolio makes it a strong prospect for financial-sustainability, costing, program-assurance, digital-delivery and workforce work.

strategic_priorities.title: Return towards profitability and sustainable pricing
strategic_priorities.description: Restore financial sustainability through service excellence, cost control, efficiency initiatives, new commercial revenue streams and a principled full-cost-recovery pricing framework agreed with the ACCC, targeting a return to profit and a dividend by FY2027-28.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Return towards profitability through service excellence, continuous refinement, cost control and sustainable pricing.

strategic_priorities.title: Upgrade infrastructure and deliver new services
strategic_priorities.description: Modernise infrastructure and stand up new services — OneSKY/CMATS, Western Sydney International and Ballina, ARFF NexGen fleet/facilities, FIMS for uncrewed aircraft, and service-level upgrades — to meet a once-in-a-generation increase in aviation capacity.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Upgrade infrastructure and deliver new services to meet the evolving needs of the aviation industry.

strategic_priorities.title: Transform organisational culture and capability
strategic_priorities.description: Accelerate cultural transformation toward a safe, inclusive, high-performing workplace; lift engagement from 59% toward 65% (FY26) and 75%; improve role clarity, leadership and internal collaboration through targeted organisational transformation initiatives.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Transform our culture through organisational health and capability enhancement.

strategic_priorities.title: Improve service efficiency and effectiveness through technology
strategic_priorities.description: Invest in next-generation technologies and future-ready infrastructure (OneSKY, ENMP, digital aerodrome services, ARFF fleet renewal) with tighter fiscal control across operating and capital expenditure.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Improve service efficiency and effectiveness through technology enhancement and transformation.

strategic_priorities.title: Build a future-ready workforce
strategic_priorities.description: Cultivate critical future skills — systems integration, data analytics, automation and cyber security — and recruit/train air traffic controllers and aviation fire fighters to address industry-wide workforce shortages.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Develop a skilled, adaptable workforce able to meet future demands.

reform_and_change_drivers: Aviation White Paper (2024) setting national priorities including airspace reform and the Aircraft Noise / Aviation Industry Ombudsman schemes
Ministerial Statement of Expectations requiring return to profitability and a dividend by FY2027-28 and independent biennial capital structure reviews
ACCC price-notification process and development of a long-term pricing agreement (FY2026-27 to FY2030-31) for full cost recovery
OneSKY/CMATS civil-military air traffic management harmonisation with Department of Defence (valued at ~$2.7bn over 20 years)
Opening of Western Sydney International Airport by end of 2026 and new services at Ballina; planning for Melbourne and Perth runways
Entry of uncrewed aircraft (UAS) and advanced air mobility, requiring FIMS and a UTM ecosystem
Net zero by 2050 commitment and Environmental Sustainability Strategy 2024-2030 (43% scope 1&2 reduction by 2030)
Full review and redesign of the Corporate Plan KPIs to strengthen legislative alignment
PFAS and asbestos contaminated-site remediation obligations exceeding current funding capacity
challenges_and_risks: Financial sustainability: EBITDA loss, NPAT loss of $115.4m forecast FY25-26, gearing at 87%, and inability to set EBITDA / cost-per-IFR-hour targets beyond one year due to uncertainty
Revenue/pricing risk: heavy reliance on traffic volumes beyond Airservices' control and ACCC-regulated pricing
PFAS remediation costs acknowledged as beyond the current funding model, likely requiring Commonwealth and airport support
Delivery risk on major transformational projects (OneSKY, WSI) and the broader change/transformation portfolio
Workforce shortages in ATC and ARFF roles in a globally competitive, STEM/cyber-heavy talent market
Cultural challenges — engagement score of 59% with an ambition of 75%
Protective security and cyber threat exposure across critical operational technology
Climate change increasing frequency/severity of weather events that disrupt services and damage infrastructure

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Committed to the Minister to return to profitability and pay a dividend by FY2027-28 while running EBITDA/NPAT losses and 87% gearing, with cost-out and efficiency initiatives across the organisation.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial-sustainability review and cost-base diagnostic supporting the return-to-profit roadmap — efficiency/cost-reduction options, scenario modelling of traffic sensitivities, and benefits tracking against the FY27-28 dividend commitment.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Priority 1 is explicitly financial recovery; the plan already uses an independent expert for financial-outlook modelling and an independent capital structure review, showing willingness to buy external financial advice.

opportunity_areas.area: Activity-based costing to underpin ACCC pricing
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Developing a long-term pricing agreement with the ACCC for full cost recovery and has introduced a new 'cost per IFR flight hour' KPI requiring robust unit-cost data.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An activity-based costing model linking service costs to the regulated price notification, defensible to the ACCC, plus support to operationalise the cost-per-IFR-flight-hour metric.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Pricing for full cost recovery and a new unit-cost KPI both require costing rigour; the plan stresses 'efficient service delivery' and benchmarking to global peers.

opportunity_areas.area: Independent assurance of OneSKY and WSI delivery
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Committed to efficient delivery of key transformational projects (OneSKY/CMATS, WSI) and states it reviews progress through internal and external assurance.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent gateway-style delivery assurance / health checks at major milestones for OneSKY and WSI, reporting to the Board Audit and Risk Committee on schedule, cost and benefits realisation risk.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names project-delivery risk as a material risk and explicitly references internal and external assurance on major projects, with a 2026 WSI go-live deadline creating time-bound demand.

opportunity_areas.area: Program/PMO and benefits management for the capital portfolio
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Running a large, concurrent capital and transformation portfolio (OneSKY, ENMP, ARFF NexGen, service-level upgrades, FIMS, WSI) under a strict prioritisation framework and tighter fiscal control.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Portfolio/PMO uplift and a benefits-management framework tying capital investments to KPIs, plus embedded delivery support on the highest-risk workstreams.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan calls out 'portfolio and project delivery capabilities' and a prioritisation framework 'directly linked to delivering against our KPIs', indicating a benefits/PMO maturity need.

opportunity_areas.area: Enterprise technology refresh and service digitalisation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Delivering enterprise technology platform modernisation, the Enterprise Network Modernisation Program, digital aerodrome services, A-CDM, DFPS and FIMS to improve resilience and customer experience.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery, service design and systems/integration delivery for the enterprise technology refresh and digital aerodrome/network programs, with business and process analysis to support service transition.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Multiple named digitalisation initiatives and an enterprise technology refresh of ERP, rostering and risk tools provide concrete, buildable scope where the firm can implement, not just advise.

opportunity_areas.area: Cyber resilience uplift for operational and enterprise technology
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Operates in an evolving threat landscape, applies a 'cyber secure by design' principle, and is investing in maturing cyber capabilities across critical CNS and business systems.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Security assessment and assurance of the modernised network/operational technology (ENMP, WSI remote tower, OneSKY) and cyber advisory to mature secure-by-design controls.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Cyber is named as a material protective-security risk and an explicit investment area; ENMP is positioned to deliver improved cyber security, creating assurance demand.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Engagement score of 59% against a 75% ambition, a cultural transformation program, operating-model changes, and shortages of ATC and ARFF staff in a competitive talent market.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Strategic workforce planning and a capability/culture uplift program — leadership development, role-clarity and organisational design support aligned to the operating-model change.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Priorities 3 and 5 and the workforce risk explicitly target culture, engagement, role clarity and critical-skills pipelines, with quantified engagement and trainee targets.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: PFAS remediation costs are acknowledged as beyond the current funding model, requiring a more suitable funding approach and engagement with the Commonwealth and airports.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A financial advisory engagement to model PFAS liability and design a sustainable, multi-party funding model (Commonwealth, airports, Airservices) with cost and liability scenarios.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan states the full cost of remediation exceeds current funding capacity and that Airservices is 'actively working with stakeholders to devise a more suitable funding model'.

opportunity_areas.area: Community and stakeholder engagement on noise/airspace change
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Increasing community scrutiny of aircraft noise; commitments under the Community Engagement Standard, NCIS response standards and ANO recommendation responses, plus engagement on flightpath/airspace changes.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Community and stakeholder engagement support for flightpath/airspace changes and noise action plans, including consultation design and content/publishing aligned to the Community Engagement Standard.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The Aviation White Paper, ANO scheme and named community-impact KPIs indicate sustained, public-facing engagement demand, particularly around WSI and Brisbane.

opportunity_areas.area: Enterprise risk and assurance framework for the change portfolio
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Notes that its risk profile shifts as it adapts its operating model and transformation portfolios, with a '3 lines' model and Board Risk Appetite Statement.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A risk-framework refresh and change-risk assurance support to embed transformation risk into the enterprise risk framework reported to the Board Audit and Risk Committee.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly flags change-program risk and the need to incorporate emerging risks into active risk management; this is a credible but more general governance need.

recommended_actions.action: Correct the account record: confirm this is Airservices Australia and re-baseline the BD plan against the Infrastructure/Transport portfolio, not Services Australia/Finance.
recommended_actions.rationale: The source metadata is wrong; pursuing the right entity, decision-makers and panels depends on fixing this first.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the CFO / finance function with a financial-sustainability and activity-based costing proposition tied to the ACCC long-term pricing agreement and the FY27-28 return-to-profit commitment.
recommended_actions.rationale: Financial recovery is Priority 1, the entity already buys independent financial modelling, and the pricing agreement is being developed now — a clear, time-bound window.

recommended_actions.action: Offer the Board Audit and Risk Committee / COO an independent delivery-assurance role across OneSKY and WSI ahead of the end-2026 WSI go-live.
recommended_actions.rationale: Project-delivery risk is named as material and the WSI deadline is fixed; assurance demand is acute and the plan references external assurance already.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a Digital & ICT delivery capability statement targeting the Enterprise Network Modernisation Program, digital aerodrome services and the enterprise technology refresh.
recommended_actions.rationale: Several named, buildable digital programs are underway where the firm can implement and integrate, differentiating from advisory-only competitors.

recommended_actions.action: Watch for the ACCC price notification, the independent capital structure review outcomes, and any PFAS funding decision as procurement triggers.
recommended_actions.rationale: Each of these is flagged as unresolved in the plan and is likely to generate downstream financial, costing and advisory work.

recommended_actions.action: Develop a workforce-and-culture pitch (engagement uplift, strategic workforce planning, operating-model design) for the Chief People Officer.
recommended_actions.rationale: Culture and workforce are explicit five-year priorities with quantified gaps, but the need is broader and less time-bound than the financial and program opportunities.

engagement_targets: Chief Financial Officer / finance function (financial sustainability, costing, ACCC pricing, PFAS funding)
Chief Operating Officer and the OneSKY and Western Sydney International program leadership
Board Audit and Risk Committee and the Chief Risk Officer (assurance, risk framework)
Chief Information/Technology Officer and Enabling Services (ENMP, enterprise technology refresh, digital aerodrome services)
Chief Information Security Officer / cyber function
Chief People Officer / Head of Workforce Planning and the culture transformation program
Communications and Media / community engagement function (noise, flightpath change)
talking_points: Your Corporate Plan commits to returning to profitability and a dividend by FY2027-28 from an EBITDA loss and 87% gearing — we can help build the cost-out roadmap and benefits tracking that makes that commitment auditable to the Board and Minister.
With a long-term ACCC pricing agreement under development and a new cost-per-IFR-flight-hour KPI, a defensible activity-based costing model is the foundation for full cost recovery.
OneSKY and WSI carry named delivery risk and a fixed 2026 deadline — independent gateway-style assurance reporting to your Audit and Risk Committee de-risks the most exposed programs.
Your enterprise technology refresh, ENMP and digital aerodrome services are buildable now — unlike advisory-only firms, we can design and implement, not just recommend.
PFAS remediation is flagged as beyond your current funding model; we can model the liability and design a multi-party funding approach for your negotiations with the Commonwealth and airports.
An engagement score of 59% against a 75% ambition during an operating-model change is exactly where structured workforce planning and culture uplift pays back.
caveats: Two important caveats. First, the source metadata is incorrect: the attached document is the Airservices Australia Corporate Plan 2025-26 (Infrastructure/Transport portfolio, a corporate Commonwealth entity), not Services Australia (Finance portfolio). This analysis is based entirely on the actual plan text. Second, the EXTERNAL RESEARCH section concerns Services Australia and largely failed/returned items about DEWR/Workforce Australia — none of it is relevant to Airservices, so news_and_controversies, anao_findings and parliamentary_matters are returned empty rather than populated with mismatched or unverified material. The opportunity assessment is well grounded in the plan, but no Airservices-specific external validation (ANAO audits on OneSKY/financial sustainability, Senate Estimates) was available; these should be researched before engagement, as OneSKY in particular has a long public history of cost and schedule scrutiny.
